body: PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA) reported a 17.5% year-over-year decline in net profit to IDR 194.17 billion for the first nine months of 2024, due to a 0.9% revenue correction and increased operational expenses. Despite this, the company remains optimistic about recording a positive trend by the end of 2024, driven by increasing demand for health checks from corporate clients and plans to implement a new business model across 89 clinic branches.
title: Prodia Laboratory Network's Net Profit Continues to Decline in First Nine Months of 2024.

body: PT Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS) will repurchase two shelf registration bonds worth IDR 350 billion, consisting of IDR 100 billion and IDR 250 billion bonds with 3-year terms and 2% yields. The buyback offer will take place next week, with the company offering 100% of the principal value, amounting to IDR 100 billion and IDR 250 billion, respectively.
title: PT Voksel Electric to Repurchase Two Shelf Registration Bonds Worth IDR 350 Billion.

body: PT Kereta Api Indonesia (KAI) will issue Shelf Registration Bonds II Phase I/2024 with a principal amount of IDR 1.5 trillion and Shelf Registration Sukuk Ijarah II Phase I/2024 with a principal amount of IDR 500 billion, each with 3 series and tenors ranging from 3 to 7 years. The proceeds from the bond issuance will be used to refinance existing bonds, while the sukuk proceeds will be used to refinance bonds and procure goods transportation infrastructure in South Sumatra.
title: PT Kereta Api Indonesia to Issue Bonds and Sukuk Worth IDR 2 Trillion for Refinancing and Infrastructure Development.

body: PT Trisula Textile Industries Tbk (TRIS) reported a net profit of IDR 12.82 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 5% year-over-year increase, driven by a 19% growth in sales to IDR 426.36 billion. The company's manufacturing business line remained the main contributor to sales, with revenues of IDR 222.68 billion, while its uniform, distribution, and retail business lines also saw significant contributions.
title: PT Trisula Textile Industries Posts 5% Year-on-Year Net Profit Growth in First Nine Months of 2024

body: PT Semen Indonesia Tbk (SMGR) reported a downturn in Q3 2024, with revenue and net profit slipping 4.93% and 58% year-on-year, respectively, to IDR 26.29 trillion and IDR 719.72 billion. The company's gross profit margin also declined to 22.87% from 26.89% in Q3 2023, driven by a decline in cement production revenue to IDR 18.99 trillion and non-cement products to IDR 729 trillion.
title: PT Semen Indonesia's revenue and net profit decline in Q3 2024 due to decreased sales and gross profit margin.

body: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk (WiFi) reported a net profit of IDR 152.07 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 3.4 times increase from the same period last year, driven by a 46% year-on-year growth in net operating revenue to IDR 504.95 billion. The company's cost revenue decreased by 4.8% year-on-year to IDR 197.36 billion, with its telecommunication business being the primary contributor to revenue, generating IDR 254.02 billion.
title: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk, also known as Wifi, reports a 3.4 times increase in net profit to IDR 152.07 billion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Jasnita Telekomindo Tbk (JAST) reported a net profit of IDR 218.7 million for the first nine months of 2024, a significant turnaround from a loss of IDR 3.2 billion in the same period last year, driven by a 19% increase in revenue to IDR 67.8 billion. The company plans to pursue existing government projects and expand into the financial institution sector, while also focusing on selling AI-based CCTV services through its subsidiary, PT Jast Indonesia Aman.
title: Jasnita Telekomindo reports net profit of IDR 218.7 million in first nine months of 2024, reversing loss from same period last year.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange listed the top 10 priciest stocks, with PT Dian Swastika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) leading the list with a closing price of IDR 44,600 per share, followed by PT DC Indonesia Tbk (DCII) with IDR 43,750 per share. The top 10 list also includes PT United Tractors Tbk (UNTR) with IDR 27,450 per share, PT Metropolitan Kentjana Tbk (MKNT) with IDR 26,000 per share, and PT Indo Tambangraya Megah Tbk (ITMG) with IDR 25,075 per share, among others.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Lists Top 10 Priciest Stocks by Closing Price

body: PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HATM) has signed a credit agreement with PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BCA) for IDR 540 billion, with a 1-year tenor, to purchase vessels supporting its core business activities. The loan, equivalent to 59.13% of HATM's total equity as of June 30, 2024, was approved by the company's general meeting of shareholders on March 15, 2024.
title: Habco Trans Maritima secures IDR 540 billion credit facility from Bank Central Asia to purchase vessels for core business activities.

body: PT Net Visi Media Tbk (NETV) saw a significant transaction with PT MD Entertainment Tbk purchasing 19.07% of the company's shares, while PT Honoris Corporindo Pratama increased its stake in PT Modernland Realty Tbk (MDLN) to 10.17%. Additionally, PT Steady Safe Tbk (SAFE) welcomed a new foreign investor, BP2S SG/BNP Paribas Singapore Branch Wealth Management, which acquired 37.15% of the company's shares, while PT Infiniti Wahana sold 42.33% of its shares in the same company.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership stakes as new investors enter the market and existing ones adjust their holdings.

body: PT Citra Development Tbk (CTRA) reported a net profit of IDR 1.27 trillion in Q3 2024, up 8.12% year-on-year, driven by an 8.01% revenue surge to IDR 7.11 trillion, with gross profit margin increasing to 48.06% from 47.35%. The company's total assets and liabilities also increased to IDR 46.51 trillion and IDR 22.97 trillion, respectively, as of September 2024.
title: PT Citra Development TBK's Net Profit Rises 8.12% to IDR 1.27 Trillion in Q3 2024.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 523.74 million shares in the Indonesian market, with PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top two sold stocks with net volumes of -202.70 million shares and -197.13 million shares, respectively. On the other hand, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) was the most bought stock with a net volume of 187.85 million shares, followed by PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk (PSAB) and PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSA) with net volumes of 23.34 million shares and 21.76 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors sell 3.12 billion shares, buy 2.60 billion shares in Indonesian market.

body: PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua Tbk (PIK) booked a net profit of IDR 487 billion in the first nine months of 2024, a 91% year-on-year increase driven by a 21% revenue jump to IDR 2.1 trillion. The company's improved performance was attributed to its efforts in establishing PIK 2 as a primary destination, with the raised capital from its recent rights issue and private placement worth IDR 17 trillion allocated to acquire more landbank.
title: Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua's net profit surges 91% to IDR 487 billion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Avia Avian TBK (AVIA) reported a net profit of IDR 1.16 trillion in Q3 2024, up from IDR 1.14 trillion in the same period last year, driven by a 4.66% year-on-year revenue increase to IDR 5.44 trillion. The company's gross profit also soared to IDR 2.40 trillion, with a higher profit margin of 44.48%, led by growth in its architecture solution segment and sales through its own distributors.
title: PT Avia Avian TBK's net profit rises to IDR 1.16 trillion in Q3 2024, driven by year-on-year revenue growth.

body: PT Sariguna Primatirta Tbk (no ticker provided) reported a 58.18% profit growth in Q3 2024, driven by a 31.53% revenue growth to IDR 1.97 trillion, with sales of bottled drinking water increasing to IDR 1.08 trillion and non-bottled water sales rising to IDR 861.98 billion. The company's gross profit margin improved to 58.57% from 55.48%, resulting in a net profit of IDR 330.95 billion, up from IDR 209.22 billion in the same period last year.
title: PT Sariguna Primatirta Sees 58.18% Profit Growth in Third Quarter 2024

body: As of October 2024, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) tops the market capitalization list with a market cap of IDR 1.26 quadrillion, accounting for around 10.04% of the total market capitalization of IDR 12.55 quadrillion. The top 10 list also includes PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk, and others, with BBCA and PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk competing for the top position in market capitalization over the first nine months of 2024.
title: PT Bank Central Asia Tops Market Capitalization List in October 2024

body: PT Samator Indo Gas Tbk (SAMATOR) reported a 2.85% revenue increase to IDR 2.12 trillion in Q3 2024, but its profit fell 27.03% to IDR 80.97 billion due to rising selling and general expenses. The company's gross profit margin decreased to 44.92%, and operating profit dropped to IDR 318.75 billion, driven by increased expenses.
title: PT Samator Indo Gas' Profit Falls 27% in Q3 2024 Despite Slight Revenue Increase

body: PT Selamat Selmata Tbk (SMSM) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 201.55 billion, or IDR 35 per share, following approval from the company's board of directors and commissioners. The dividend payment will be made on November 21, 2024, to shareholders recorded on November 11, 2024, with the company having recorded a net profit of IDR 719.37 billion in the first nine months of 2024.
title: PT Selamat Sempurna Tbk to Distribute IDR 201.55 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL) reported a 14.12% increase in net income to IDR 17.24 trillion in the first nine months of 2024, driven by a 73.18% contribution from hardware sales, which reached IDR 12.62 trillion. The company's gross profit margin decreased to 7.86% from 8.16% in the same period last year, while net profit rose 13.34% to IDR 3455.41 billion.
title: PT Metrodata Electronics Posts 14.12% Revenue Growth in First Nine Months of 2024

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) has distributed IDR 77.3 trillion in credit to the micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSME) sector as of September 2024, with IDR 36.6 trillion in subsidized credit and IDR 40.7 trillion in non-subsidized credit, demonstrating its commitment to supporting economic growth. BNI's sustainability portfolio has reached IDR 188 trillion, accounting for 26% of its total credit, as the bank prioritizes environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles in its business practices.
title: BNI Commits to Empowering Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises through Funding Support and Sustainability Initiatives.

body: Prudential Indonesia (PRUD) aims to expand its agent distribution network, particularly among the younger generation, with over 100,000 agents, 52% of whom are millennials and 19% Gen Z, as of June 2024. The company sees huge potential in the insurance agency business, with a growth rate of new customer acquisition primarily driven by the younger segment, which currently accounts for under 5% of Prudential Indonesia's users.
title: Prudential Indonesia Hosts NextGen Fest 2024 to Attract Younger Generation to Insurance Industry

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has begun evaluating 16 indices, including IDX30, LQ45, and IDX80, from November 1, 2024, to January 31, 2025, resulting in changes to constituents, with additions including PT Mitra Adiperkasa Tbk (MITA) and PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBBM), and removals including PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) and PT Indo Tambangraya Megah Tbk (ITMG). The evaluations also led to changes in other indices, such as BISNIS-27, MNC36, and SMINFRA18, with new additions and removals of constituents.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Conducts Major Evaluations on Six Indices and Minor Evaluations on 10 Others.

body: PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BARA) increased its stake in PT Chandra Asri Pacific to 13.67% by purchasing over 2.63 billion shares, while other notable transactions included PT Honoris Corporindo Pratama buying 342.15 million shares of PT Modernland Realty Tbk (MDLN) and DBS Bank Ltd S/A PT Bank Keb Hana Indonesia acquiring 5.44 million shares of PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk (LPKR). Conversely, Winfly Ltd. sold 256.35 million shares of PT Bank MNC Internasional Tbk (BABP), and other investors made notable sales, including HSBC Bank Plc selling 2 million shares of PT Apexindo Pratama Duta Tbk (APEX).
title: Multiple companies and investors engage in significant share transactions in Indonesian market.

body: PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) will close down some of its business lines as part of a restructuring plan, with the goal of completing the closure of several subsidiaries by H2 2025. The company, which debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange in 2021, has acknowledged that its business performance has changed significantly post-IPO, and has spent a large sum of money to support its business, both organically and inorganically.
title: Bukalapak to Restructure and Close Some Business Lines to Improve Performance.

body: Newton Capital Ltd. acquired a 10.67% ownership stake in PT Net Visi Media Tbk (NETV) by purchasing over 4.41 billion shares, while PT Startel Communication acquired a 24.02% stake in PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk (JAYA) by buying 900,000 shares. Meanwhile, several companies, including PT Nippon Indosari Corpindo Tbk (ROSI) and PT Lautan Luas Tbk (LTLS), conducted share buybacks, and individual investors and foreign investors made notable transactions in various companies, including PT Dewi Sri Farmindo Tbk (DESF) and PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk (VKTR).
title: PT Net Visi Media TBK welcomes new investor Newton Capital Ltd with 10.67% ownership stake, among other market transactions.

body: Foreign investors' net selling volume reached -409.17 million shares, driven by sales of PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (GOTO) shares (-115.75 million shares) and banking sector shares, including PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK (BBRI) (-85.48 million shares) and PT Bank KB Bukopin TBK (BBKP) (-31.63 million shares). Meanwhile, foreign investors bought shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (BIPI) (34.45 million shares), PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK (33.08 million shares), and PT Japfa Comfeed Indonesia TBK (JPFA) (26.39 million shares), among others.
title: Foreign investors' net selling reaches 409 million shares in Indonesian market.

body: On Monday, October 28, net foreign volume in Indonesia's stock market returned to the green zone, with a total of 3.78 billion shares purchased and 3.64 billion shares sold, resulting in a net volume of 143.63 million shares. The top purchased shares included PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 1.54 billion shares, while the top sold shares included PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) with 309.98 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market purchase shares of digital and mining companies, sell shares of e-commerce and banking institutions on Monday.

body: PT Dharma Satya Nusantara Tbk (DSNG) reported a 9% year-over-year increase in revenue to IDR 7.2 trillion, driven by higher selling prices of palm oil products, despite a 5.1% decline in fresh fruit bunches production. The company's net profit also jumped 72% year-over-year to IDR 868.33 trillion, with solid financials, including a 7.8% increase in assets to IDR 17.4 trillion.
title: Dharma Satya Nusantara's revenue and net profit increase despite decline in palm oil production due to rising selling prices and falling fertilizer costs.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange listed the top 10 issuers with the highest number of shares, led by PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 1.20 trillion listed shares and a market capitalization of IDR 84.10 trillion. PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) had the highest market capitalization of IDR 893.02 trillion, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with IDR 703.70 trillion, and PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with IDR 53.03 trillion.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Lists Top 10 Issuers with Highest Number of Shares, Led by PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK

body: The Indonesian government's latest securities auction received total incoming bids of IDR 29.58 trillion, with the highest bids in the SPN category totaling IDR 3.52 trillion and the FR0103 category receiving IDR 5.7 trillion in bids. The total awarded amount from the 8 series offered was IDR 18.85 trillion, bringing the total awarded amount from 20 auctions since the beginning of 2024 to IDR 459.46 trillion.
title: Indonesia's Government Securities Auction Sees Total Bids of IDR 29.58 Trillion
